summary: codebuff-cli impersonates the codebuff CLI and runs an opaque binary from an unrelated GitHub account
details: The package name and README impersonate the legitimate codebuff CLI (published by Manicode/CodebuffAI), but the bin entry's launcher (cli/bin/codebuff.cjs) downloads a platform-specific native executable from github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases — a personal GitHub account unrelated to the upstream publisher. The launcher constructs the release URL from a hardcoded REPO constant ('Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli'), writes the fetched bytes to ~/.codebuff/bin/codebuff, chmods 0755, and spawns it with the user's argv with no hash, signature, or publisher verification. A second copy of the same downloader logic ships in cli/scripts/download-binary.cjs. No npm lifecycle hook is declared, so the fetch fires only when an installer follows the README and runs the CLI; at that moment, arbitrary native code controlled by the unrelated GitHub owner executes on the user's machine. Two independent block signals are present: name impersonation of an established publisher, and a publisher-mismatched binary dropper with no integrity check.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
